DmRegisterCommandProcessorEx

Registers a command handler, optionally allowing the handler to be assigned to run in a particular thread.

DMHRAPI DmRegisterCommandProcessorEx(
    LPCSTR szProcessor,
    PDM_CMDPROC pfn,
    PVOID pfnCreateThread
);

Parameters

szProcessor
LPCSTR that represents the command processor.
pfn
Pointer to a command processor. Command processors must take the following form:
HRESULT __stdcall my_handler(LPCSTR szCommand, LPSTR szResponse, DWORD cchResponse, PDM_CMDCONT pdmcc);
pfnCreateThread
Pointer to the handle for a thread in which the command processor will run. If NULL is passed for this parameter, the command handler runs in the global server thread.

Return Values

Returns XBDM_NOERR if successful, an error value otherwise.

Remarks

DmRegisterCommandProcessorEx may be used to register either threaded or non-threaded command handlers. Passing a pointer to a thread handle for the pfnCreateThread parameter registers a threaded command handler. Passing NULL for the pfnCreateThread parameter registers a non-threaded command handler.

Non-threaded command handlers cannot call CRT or XAPI functions. On the other hand, threaded command handlers can call CRT and XAPI functions because they run in a separate thread from the global server thread. Threaded command handlers run only on XAPI threads, not on the global server thread.

A non-threaded command handler may also be registered with the DmRegisterCommandProcessor function. A threaded command handler may also be registered with the DmRegisterThreadedCommandProcessor function, which also creates a new thread in which the command handler runs.

When a title first opens a connection with DmOpenConnection, the title may use that connection only to invoke either built-in commands or commands directed at non-threaded handlers. Any attempt to invoke a threaded handler results in the error XBDM_MUSTBEDEDICATED. To invoke a command directed at a threaded handler, the title must first dedicate that connection to a specific handler by calling DmDedicateConnection. The call to DmDedicateConnection should follow the title's call to DmOpenConnection.

Once a connection has been dedicated, the connection may be used only to invoke commands directed at the specific dedicated handler. Any attempt to use a dedicated connection to invoke either built-in commands or commands directed at non-threaded handlers results in the error XBDM_MUSTBEDEDICATED. A title may re-dedicate a connection to another handler at any time by calling DmDedicateConnection again. Passing NULL for the szHandler parameter to DmDedicateConnection re-dedicates the connection to the global server thread. Re-dedicating this connection allows the connection to once again be used for built-in commands.

See the Remarks section of DmRegisterCommandProcessor for further details on registering a command processor.

Requirements

Header: Declared in XbDm.h.

Import Library: Use XbDm.lib.

See Also

DmDedicateConnection, DmRegisterCommandProcessor, DmRegisterThreadedCommandProcessor
